Had quite a lot of fun.
A cipher with only three lines?
"Erh, I think this is an audio file?"
The problem involves the determinant of a 5 by 5 matrix, where we can decide certain the value of cells, and some other values unknown.
Had quite a lot of fun playing the crypto challenges, they were just the right difficulty for me, not to hard.

After reading through the challenge, we can see that the encryption method simply adds numbers from a[i] where ith bit is 1.
This means its sort of .
We are allowed to change the 5 cells of a 5 by 5 diagonal matrix.

We observe that the flag is encrypted with XOR with a cycling key of length 8.
And since we already know 8 characters of the flag (which happens to be length 48), we can determine the key by cipher ^ plain = key.
We are not allowed to use the following expressions in python: assign, call, import, import from, binary operation (+-/ etc).
Very hard challenges, would love if there was more time for me to learn assembly.

Though web is my preferred category, this CTF's web challenges are really hard.
I even made a whole new website to host my attack payloads in the process.
I was able to get a string __import__ but that's it.
I don't know AES, but I know brute force.
The big number can be factorized with online tools Integer factorization calculator (alpertron.com.ar)
It appears that every character is replaced by their MD5 hash.
QR Code repair, tedious and painful.
First time doing anything RTP related, fun challenge.

The image is suspiciously large for its pixel size.
It seems that the validation step would take a very  long time, which means its gonna be timing attack!
Command injection, except I can't use commands, because its 8 char max.
